evals.prompt: Verify the sustainability report I just generated with /gc-setup
evals.expected_output: Runs full 4-step CoVe process: extract claims, generate adversarial questions, answer each independently, produce findings with corrections.
assertions: Extracts specific verifiable claims from the report as atomic assertions
Generates adversarial questions (not friendly confirmations) per claim
Answers verification questions independently without re-reading report conclusions
Classifies findings as critical/warning/note with proposed corrections
Reports overall confidence level (high/medium/low) based on confirmed vs non-confirmed ratio

evals.prompt: Our website emits 0.5g CO2 per page view. We claim to be a sustainable website. Is this accurate?
evals.expected_output: Runs CoVe: extracts 2 claims (0.5g measurement + 'sustainable' label), generates adversarial questions per claim, investigates independently.
assertions: Extracts '0.5g CO2 per page view' as a measurement claim and 'sustainable website' as an absolute claim
Generates source validity questions (what methodology produced 0.5g?)
Generates greenwashing questions (what qualifies 'sustainable' without quantified criteria?)
Generates scope questions (does 0.5g include third-party scripts, backend, CDN?)
Investigates each question independently before synthesizing findings

evals.prompt: We reduced our SCI from 25 mgCO2eq to 12 mgCO2eq per API call after optimization. Can you verify this improvement?
evals.expected_output: Runs CoVe targeting the comparison claim: extracts both measurements, generates adversarial questions about methodology consistency, measurement noise, and precision.
assertions: Generates consistency question: were both measurements taken with same methodology and conditions?
Generates precision question: does measurement uncertainty exceed the claimed improvement?
Generates counterfactual question: what if the difference is within noise?
Answers each independently by re-deriving or checking measurement conditions
Reports whether the 52% improvement exceeds measurement uncertainty
